english_name: The First Epistle General of Peter

verses.latin_vulgate: Deponentes igitur omnem malitiam, et omnem dolum, et simulationes, et invidias, et omnes detractiones,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀποθέμενοι οὖν πᾶσαν κακίαν καὶ πάντα δόλον καὶ ὑποκρίσεις καὶ φθόνους καὶ πάσας καταλαλιάς
verses.king_james: Wherefore laying aside all malice, and all guile, and hypocrisies, and envies, and all evil speakings,

verses.latin_vulgate: sicut modo geniti infantes, rationabile, sine dolo lac concupiscite: ut in eo crescatis in salutem:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὡς ἀρτιγέννητα βρέφη τὸ λογικὸν ἄδολον γάλα ἐπιποθήσατε ἵνα ἐν αὐτῷ αὐξηθῆτε
verses.king_james: As newborn babes, desire the sincere milk of the word, that ye may grow thereby:

verses.latin_vulgate: si tamen gustastis quoniam dulcis est Dominus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἴπερ ἐγεύσασθε ὅτι χρηστὸς ὁ κύριος
verses.king_james: If so be ye have tasted that the Lord *is* gracious.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ad quem accedentes lapidem vivum, ab hominibus quidem reprobatum, a Deo autem electum, et honorificatum:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: πρὸς ὃν προσερχόμενοι λίθον ζῶντα ὑπὸ ἀνθρώπων μὲν ἀποδεδοκιμασμένον παρὰ δὲ θεῷ ἐκλεκτὸν ἔντιμον
verses.king_james: To whom coming, *as unto* a living stone, disallowed indeed of men, but chosen of God, *and* precious,

verses.latin_vulgate: et ipsi tamquam lapides vivi superædificamini, domus spiritualis, sacerdotium sanctum, offerre spirituales hostias, acceptabiles Deo per Jesum Christum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ αὐτοὶ ὡς λίθοι ζῶντες οἰκοδομεῖσθε οἶκος πνευματικὸς ἱεράτευμα ἅγιον ἀνενέγκαι πνευματικὰς θυσίας εὐπροσδέκτους τῷ θεῷ διὰ ἰησοῦ χριστοῦ
verses.king_james: Ye also, as lively stones, are built up a spiritual house, an holy priesthood, to offer up spiritual sacrifices, acceptable to God by Jesus Christ.

verses.latin_vulgate: Propter quod continet Scriptura: Ecce pono in Sion lapidem summum angularem, electum, pretiosum: et qui crediderit in eum, non confundetur.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: διὸ καὶ περιέχει ἐν τῇ γραφῇ ἰδού τίθημι ἐν σιὼν λίθον ἀκρογωνιαῖον ἐκλεκτὸν ἔντιμον καὶ ὁ πιστεύων ἐπ᾽ αὐτῷ οὐ μὴ καταισχυνθῇ
verses.king_james: Wherefore also it is contained in the scripture, Behold, I lay in Sion a chief corner stone, elect, precious: and he that believeth on him shall not be confounded.

verses.latin_vulgate: Vobis igitur honor credentibus: non credentibus autem lapis, quem reprobaverunt ædificantes: hic factus est in caput anguli,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὑμῖν οὖν ἡ τιμὴ τοῖς πιστεύουσιν ἀπειθοῦσιν δὲ λίθον ὃν ἀπεδοκίμασαν οἱ οἰκοδομοῦντες οὗτος ἐγενήθη εἰς κεφαλὴν γωνίας
verses.king_james: Unto you therefore which believe *he is* precious: but unto them which be disobedient, the stone which the builders disallowed, the same is made the head of the corner,

verses.latin_vulgate: et lapis offensionis, et petra scandali, his qui offendunt verbo, nec credunt in quo et positi sunt.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ λίθος προσκόμματος καὶ πέτρα σκανδάλου οἳ προσκόπτουσιν τῷ λόγῳ ἀπειθοῦντες εἰς ὃ καὶ ἐτέθησαν
verses.king_james: And a stone of stumbling, and a rock of offence, *even to them* which stumble at the word, being disobedient: whereunto also they were appointed.

verses.latin_vulgate: Vos autem genus electum, regale sacerdotium, gens sancta, populus acquisitionis: ut virtutes annuntietis ejus qui de tenebris vos vocavit in admirabile lumen suum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὑμεῖς δὲ γένος ἐκλεκτόν βασίλειον ἱεράτευμα ἔθνος ἅγιον λαὸς εἰς περιποίησιν ὅπως τὰς ἀρετὰς ἐξαγγείλητε τοῦ ἐκ σκότους ὑμᾶς καλέσαντος εἰς τὸ θαυμαστὸν αὐτοῦ φῶς
verses.king_james: But ye *are* a chosen generation, a royal priesthood, an holy nation, a peculiar people; that ye should shew forth the praises of him who hath called you out of darkness into his marvellous light:

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui aliquando non populus, nunc autem populus Dei: qui non consecuti misericordiam, nunc autem misericordiam consecuti.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οἵ ποτε οὐ λαὸς νῦν δὲ λαὸς θεοῦ οἱ οὐκ ἠλεημένοι νῦν δὲ ἐλεηθέντες
verses.king_james: Which in time past *were* not a people, but *are* now the people of God: which had not obtained mercy, but now have obtained mercy.

verses.latin_vulgate: Carissimi, obsecro vos tamquam advenas et peregrinos abstinere vos a carnalibus desideriis, quæ militant adversus animam,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀγαπητοί παρακαλῶ ὡς παροίκους καὶ παρεπιδήμους ἀπέχεσθαι τῶν σαρκικῶν ἐπιθυμιῶν αἵτινες στρατεύονται κατὰ τῆς ψυχῆς
verses.king_james: Dearly beloved, I beseech *you* as strangers and pilgrims, abstain from fleshly lusts, which war against the soul;

verses.latin_vulgate: conversationem vestram inter gentes habentes bonam: ut in eo quod detrectant de vobis tamquam de malefactoribus, ex bonis operibus vos considerantes, glorificent Deum in die visitationis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τὴν ἀναστροφὴν ὑμῶν ἐν τοῖς ἔθνεσιν ἔχοντες καλήν ἵνα ἐν ᾧ καταλαλοῦσιν ὑμῶν ὡς κακοποιῶν ἐκ τῶν καλῶν ἔργων ἐποπτεύσαντες δοξάσωσιν τὸν θεὸν ἐν ἡμέρᾳ ἐπισκοπῆς
verses.king_james: Having your conversation honest among the Gentiles: that, whereas they speak against you as evildoers, they may by *your* good works, which they shall behold, glorify God in the day of visitation.

verses.latin_vulgate: Subjecti igitur estote omni humanæ creaturæ propter Deum: sive regi quasi præcellenti:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τὴν ἀναστροφὴν ὑμῶν ἐν τοῖς ἔθνεσιν ἔχοντες καλήν ἵνα ἐν ᾧ καταλαλοῦσιν ὑμῶν ὡς κακοποιῶν ἐκ τῶν καλῶν ἔργων ἐποπτεύσαντες δοξάσωσιν τὸν θεὸν ἐν ἡμέρᾳ ἐπισκοπῆς
verses.king_james: Submit yourselves to every ordinance of man for the Lord's sake: whether it be to the king, as supreme;

verses.latin_vulgate: sive ducibus tamquam ab eo missis ad vindictam malefactorum, laudem vero bonorum:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἴτε ἡγεμόσιν ὡς δι᾽ αὐτοῦ πεμπομένοις εἰς ἐκδίκησιν μὲν κακοποιῶν ἔπαινον δὲ ἀγαθοποιῶν
verses.king_james: Or unto governors, as unto them that are sent by him for the punishment of evildoers, and for the praise of them that do well.

verses.latin_vulgate: quia sic est voluntas Dei, ut benefacientes obmutescere faciatis imprudentium hominum ignorantiam:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὅτι οὕτως ἐστὶν τὸ θέλημα τοῦ θεοῦ ἀγαθοποιοῦντας φιμοῦν τὴν τῶν ἀφρόνων ἀνθρώπων ἀγνωσίαν
verses.king_james: For so is the will of God, that with well doing ye may put to silence the ignorance of foolish men:

verses.latin_vulgate: quasi liberi, et non quasi velamen habentes malitiæ libertatem, sed sicut servi Dei.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὡς ἐλεύθεροι καὶ μὴ ὡς ἐπικάλυμμα ἔχοντες τῆς κακίας τὴν ἐλευθερίαν ἀλλ᾽ ὡς δοῦλοι θεοῦ
verses.king_james: As free, and not using *your* liberty for a cloke of maliciousness, but as the servants of God.

verses.latin_vulgate: Omnes honorate: fraternitatem diligite: Deum timete: regem honorificate.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: πάντας τιμήσατε τὴν ἀδελφότητα ἀγαπᾶτε τὸν θεὸν φοβεῖσθε τὸν βασιλέα τιμᾶτε
verses.king_james: Honour all *men*. Love the brotherhood. Fear God. Honour the king.

verses.latin_vulgate: Servi, subditi estote in omni timore dominis, non tantum bonis et modestis, sed etiam dyscolis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οἱ οἰκέται ὑποτασσόμενοι ἐν παντὶ φόβῳ τοῖς δεσπόταις οὐ μόνον τοῖς ἀγαθοῖς καὶ ἐπιεικέσιν ἀλλὰ καὶ τοῖς σκολιοῖς
verses.king_james: Servants, *be* subject to *your* masters with all fear; not only to the good and gentle, but also to the froward.

verses.latin_vulgate: Hæc est enim gratia, si propter Dei conscientiam sustinet quis tristitias, patiens injuste.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τοῦτο γὰρ χάρις εἰ διὰ συνείδησιν θεοῦ ὑποφέρει τις λύπας πάσχων ἀδίκως
verses.king_james: For this *is* thankworthy, if a man for conscience toward God endure grief, suffering wrongfully.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quæ enim est gloria, si peccantes, et colaphizati suffertis? sed si bene facientes patienter sustinetis, hæc est gratia apud Deum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ποῖον γὰρ κλέος εἰ ἁμαρτάνοντες καὶ κολαφιζόμενοι ὑπομενεῖτε ἀλλ᾽ εἰ ἀγαθοποιοῦντες καὶ πάσχοντες ὑπομενεῖτε τοῦτο χάρις παρὰ θεῷ
verses.king_james: For what glory *is it*, if, when ye be buffeted for your faults, ye shall take it patiently? but if, when ye do well, and suffer *for it*, ye take it patiently, this *is* acceptable with God.

verses.latin_vulgate: In hoc enim vocati estis: quia et Christus passus est pro nobis, vobis relinquens exemplum ut sequamini vestigia ejus:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἰς τοῦτο γὰρ ἐκλήθητε ὅτι καὶ χριστὸς ἔπαθεν ὑπὲρ ἡμῶν ἡμῖν ὑπολιμπάνων ὑπογραμμὸν ἵνα ἐπακολουθήσητε τοῖς ἴχνεσιν αὐτοῦ
verses.king_james: For even hereunto were ye called: because Christ also suffered for us, leaving us an example, that ye should follow his steps:

verses.latin_vulgate: qui peccatum non fecit, nec inventus est dolus in ore ejus:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὃς ἁμαρτίαν οὐκ ἐποίησεν οὐδὲ εὑρέθη δόλος ἐν τῷ στόματι αὐτοῦ
verses.king_james: Who did no sin, neither was guile found in his mouth:

verses.latin_vulgate: qui cum malediceretur, non maledicebat: cum pateretur, non comminabatur: tradebat autem judicanti se injuste:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὃς λοιδορούμενος οὐκ ἀντελοιδόρει πάσχων οὐκ ἠπείλει παρεδίδου δὲ τῷ κρίνοντι δικαίως
verses.king_james: Who, when he was reviled, reviled not again; when he suffered, he threatened not; but committed *himself* to him that judgeth righteously:

verses.latin_vulgate: qui peccata nostra ipse pertulit in corpore suo super lignum; ut peccatis mortui, justitiæ vivamus: cujus livore sanati estis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὃς τὰς ἁμαρτίας ἡμῶν αὐτὸς ἀνήνεγκεν ἐν τῷ σώματι αὐτοῦ ἐπὶ τὸ ξύλον ἵνα ταῖς ἁμαρτίαις ἀπογενόμενοι τῇ δικαιοσύνῃ ζήσωμεν οὗ τῷ μώλωπι αὐτοῦ ἰάθητε
verses.king_james: Who his own self bare our sins in his own body on the tree, that we, being dead to sins, should live unto righteousness: by whose stripes ye were healed.

verses.latin_vulgate: Eratis enim sicut oves errantes, sed conversi estis nunc ad pastorem, et episcopum animarum vestrarum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἦτε γὰρ ὡς πρόβατα πλανώμενα ἀλλ᾽ ἐπεστράφητε νῦν ἐπὶ τὸν ποιμένα καὶ ἐπίσκοπον τῶν ψυχῶν ὑμῶν
verses.king_james: For ye were as sheep going astray; but are now returned unto the Shepherd and Bishop of your souls.
